title: Machine Coding — Kanban Board
description: Today's assigned project (randomly selected from the interview pool): build a Kanban Board in 90 minutes with TypeScript, API integration, clean architecture, and full UI states. Treat this exactly like a live machine coding round — clarify requirements, plan structure, ship a working MVP, then iterate.
requirements: Three columns: To Do, In Progress, Done — each displays task cards
Fetch tasks from a REST API (JSONPlaceholder /todos or custom mock API)
Add new task with title via form input
Move task between columns (buttons acceptable — drag-and-drop is bonus)
TypeScript interfaces for Task, Column, and API responses
Feature-based folder structure: components, hooks, api, types
Loading skeleton while fetching initial tasks
Error UI with retry on API failure
Empty state per column when no tasks exist
Responsive layout — usable on mobile and desktop
No complete solution copy-paste — build from scratch under time pressure
acceptanceCriteria: Interviewer can add a task and see it in To Do within 90 minutes
Moving a task updates UI immediately with immutable state updates
API failure shows error panel; retry recovers without page refresh
Empty columns show helpful message, not blank space
npm run build passes with strict TypeScript
No infinite re-renders in Network or React DevTools
Folder structure is explainable in under 60 seconds
Candidate can articulate trade-offs when asked
hints: Spend first 5 minutes clarifying scope with your imaginary interviewer: move via buttons is fine.
Map API todos to Kanban columns using status field or local column assignment.
Separate fetchTasks() in api/ — keep page component under 80 lines.
Use a single tasks state array; derive column contents with filter — avoid three separate arrays that drift.
Start with static columns and local state; wire API second if time is tight.
Announce your plan out loud — interviewers score communication.

bonus.title: Interview impress
bonus.description: Only if core requirements ship before the 90-minute bell.
requirements: Filter tasks by search input with debounce
Optimistic UI on task move with rollback on failure

debuggingScenarios.title: Infinite Rendering
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Browser freezes; React DevTools shows thousands of renders; Network tab floods
debuggingScenarios.cause: useEffect calls setState without dependency array, or setState triggers effect that sets same state again
debuggingScenarios.fix: Add correct dependency array; guard effect with condition; never set state unconditionally on every render

debuggingScenarios.title: State Mutation
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Moving a task does not re-render; React DevTools state looks updated but UI is stale
debuggingScenarios.cause: tasks.push() or column.tasks = newTasks mutates state array in place instead of spreading into new array
debuggingScenarios.fix: setTasks(prev => prev.map(t => t.id === id ? { ...t, status } : t)) — always return new references

debuggingScenarios.title: Missing Dependency
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Stale task list after filter change; effect never re-fetches when searchQuery updates
debuggingScenarios.cause: useEffect(() => fetchTasks(query), []) — empty deps ignore query changes
debuggingScenarios.fix: Include searchQuery in dependency array or move fetch to event handler; run exhaustive-deps lint

debuggingScenarios.title: Wrong Key
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Deleting one task removes wrong card; input focus jumps between rows; state mixes up after reorder
debuggingScenarios.cause: Using array index as key in tasks.map((task, i) => <Card key={i} />)
debuggingScenarios.fix: Use stable unique id: key={task.id}. Never use index for dynamic lists with add/remove/reorder

debuggingScenarios.title: API Failure
debuggingScenarios.symptom: White screen or uncaught promise rejection when API returns 500; no user feedback
debuggingScenarios.cause: Missing try/catch; assuming fetch throws on HTTP errors; not checking response.ok
debuggingScenarios.fix: Wrap async in try/catch; check response.ok; setError state; render ErrorPanel with retry handler

debuggingScenarios.title: Routing Bug
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Navigating to /board/123 shows blank page; useParams returns undefined; 404 on refresh
debuggingScenarios.cause: Route defined as /board/:id but link goes to /boards/123; or missing catch-all route; SPA not configured for production
debuggingScenarios.fix: Align path strings between Route path and Link to; type useParams; add NotFound route; verify vite base
